title: Clocks Do Not Own Time
why_it_might_be_new: The nearest neighbors already deny that clocks measure a substance called time. Relational time, emergent clock-time, and Dogen-block-universe comparisons all reach parts of the claim. The exact difference is narrower: a successful timing method should be trusted for coordination but refused as a ruler over metaphysics or personal worth. Existing prior art asks what time is, whether all moments exist, or how clock-time can be reconstructed. This finding asks what a working method is allowed to authorize, then ties that limit to a modern wound: people letting schedules and future projections act as verdicts on the self.

critique: The strongest objection is that physics does not need any holder for clocks to tick. A cesium atom oscillates whether anyone holds anything; a binary pulsar keeps time independent of attention. Calling the clock parasitic on distention may smuggle phenomenology into a regime where only relations between systems are doing work. Heidegger and Husserl are uncomfortably close: if ecstatic temporality already says what this finding says, the novelty collapses into a Lumenary-flavored repackaging plus a modern-anxiety frame. Dogen would press harder: holding past, attention, and future may still imply a holder outside the holding, which Uji denies. Nagarjuna in MMK 19 would press from the other side: present, past, and future cannot stand as independent terms at all, so the recommended stretch may reify exactly the three modes the analysis is supposed to dissolve. The model should be weakened if practitioners report that practicing a stretched present increases anxiety, dissociation, or planning compulsion, or if it merely produces another performance of self-management for the very cohort that already over-manages.
next_research_directions: If this model is right, then teaching a stretched present that explicitly carries memory and intention should reduce both rumination and future dread together for the same person. If only one falls and the other rises, the holding frame is splitting rather than uniting, and the model is weakened.

If this model is right, then practitioners trained in narrative contemplative practices (Examen, Jewish daily review, lectio divina, Sufi recollection) should integrate past, attention, and future more easily than practitioners trained in pure present-instant practices, when both are placed under deadline pressure. If pure-present practitioners do equally well, the stretched-present claim adds nothing beyond ordinary mindfulness.

If this model is right, then a physics teaching that introduces clocks as coordination devices rather than time-revealing instruments should reduce the common misreading that special relativity proves the felt present is illusory. If students still draw the illusion conclusion at the same rate, the operational frame has no traction.

Close-read Husserl's lectures on inner time consciousness, Heidegger Being and Time sections 65-66, and Bergson's Time and Free Will against Augustine's Confessions XI to test whether the stretched-holding claim survives or collapses into ecstatic temporality with new wording.

Run Nagarjuna MMK 19 as the hardest pressure: if the present has no independent existence, then keeping past, attention, and future distinct may be exactly the conceptual error to dissolve. Revise the doctrine candidate to a pragmatic instruction rather than a metaphysical claim if MMK 19 succeeds.

Protocol improvement: every future run that uses a phenomenological method as a thinking lens must pair it with at least one operational or relational physical account before letting lived structure carry cosmological weight.
original_claim: Every clock externalizes what it cannot itself contain: a held stretch of remembered past, attended present, and intended future, kept distinct without being separated. Physics can give the reading at coincidence and the relation between systems; it cannot give the holding that makes a reading legible as a reading. Augustine called this holding the distended soul. Plotinus called the unstretched form of it eternity. Dogen refused the very separation of holder and held. The block universe does not erase the holding, because any view of the block must be held by someone who keeps past, present, and future distinct enough to view them as past, present, and future. The felt present is therefore neither an illusion inside a finished block nor the only thing that is real. It is the place where the block becomes participable: the site where a person can keep a promise to someone gone, attend to what is in front of them, and act for someone not yet here. To live well in time is to hold the stretch honestly. Anxiety strips the holding to a thin now and complains the now is too small to live in. Rumination drowns the holding in past without attention. Future dread drowns it in expectation without memory or care. Mindfulness misread as collapse into the instant cuts the stretch from the other side. The healthy form is neither thinning nor dispersal: it is the disciplined width of a present that still carries and still intends.

practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the practice becomes a ritualized planning compulsion, increases rumination, raises shame, or pulls attention away from urgent action that real life is asking for. Use clinical and human support where the wound is larger than a practice can hold.
practice_candidate.frequency: Once daily, plus once before a known pressure moment, for two weeks.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for acute panic, intrusive trauma memory, severe depression, mania, psychosis, addiction withdrawal, or active suicidal ideation. Not a substitute for sleep, therapy, medication, planning systems, or honest conversation with the people involved. Not for situations that require immediate action with no time to reflect.
notice: Whether holding all three relieves the felt thinness of the present, or makes it more crowded.
Whether the next ordinary action becomes more obvious or more avoidable.
Whether attention quietly returns to the people and duties already in your life.
Whether the exercise becomes another performance of self-management.
practice_candidate.practice_line: Name what you carry, where you are, and what you move toward; then do one ordinary thing that fits.
practice_candidate.purpose: To replace anxious collapse into a thin instant, dispersal into past or future, and self-management as identity, with a held present that honors memory, attention, and intention without forcing resolution.
steps: Sit or stand. Set a soft 5 minute limit. Do not watch the clock during the practice.
Name one thing you carry from before: a kindness, an unfinished work, a wound being healed, a lesson learned.
Name where you are right now: this room, this body, this breath, the responsibility actually in front of you.
Name one thing you move toward: a person, a task, a meal, an obligation, a rest you have earned.
Hold the three together for one slow breath. Do not collapse them into a single phrase. Do not force them into a story.
Choose one small ordinary action for the next hour that fits the stretch: a message sent, a task finished, a meal made, a conversation begun, a body laid down.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: Adults under deadline pressure who already use some reflective practice and notice that pure present-moment instructions thin the moment rather than steadying it. Useful for caregivers, founders, students before exams, and people preparing for a difficult conversation or task.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Time anxiety, rumination, future dread, and the habit of treating the present as a verdict on the self.
practice_candidate.title: The Stretched Now
practice_candidate.weakens_if: After two weeks it produces more self-monitoring, more avoidance of duties, more spiritual pride, or no detectable difference from ordinary journaling or ordinary planning.

source_basis: Mode: Discovery mode. The frontier on method authority is mature; the time seed needs a positive claim that survives both physics and contemplative pressure rather than another stage of authority audit.

Thinking method source: Augustine, Confessions XI.20-28 on the distended soul. I held past, attention, and future as one stretched act and watched what each tradition says about whether the holder can step outside the holding. Critique of the method: distention can make the human soul look load-bearing in cosmology when it may only be load-bearing in lived experience.

Contrasting thinking method source: Dogen, Shobogenzo Uji, source card notes/source-cards/buddhism-dogen-uji.md. Being and time are not two; there is no holder outside the holding. This corrects Augustine's bias toward a soul that performs the stretch.

Primary physics: Einstein, On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies, section 1, where time is defined for distant events through clocks and light signals, https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/On_the_Electrodynamics_of_Moving_Bodies_(1920_edition). The definition is operational: time is the reading of a clock at a coincidence.

Primary physics: Einstein's 1955 letter to the family of Michele Besso, in which he writes that the distinction between past, present, and future is for us believing physicists a stubbornly persistent illusion. Cited and discussed in Carlo Rovelli, The Order of Time, Riverhead 2018, chapter 1.

Primary physics: Rovelli and Carlo, Partial Observables, Phys Rev D 65 (2002), https://arxiv.org/abs/gr-qc/0110035. Time in fundamental physics is a relation between observables, not an independent parameter.

Primary physics-philosophy: Page and Wootters, Evolution Without Evolution, Phys Rev D 27 (1983), https://cds.cern.ch/record/143641. Time emerges from correlations between subsystems in a globally timeless state.

Primary contemplative: Plotinus, Enneads III.7, On Eternity and Time. Eternity is not endless duration; it is life all at once, complete. Time is the moving image of eternity. https://www.sacred-texts.com/cla/plotenn/enn326.htm

Primary contemplative: Nagarjuna, Mulamadhyamakakarika chapter 19 on time, especially the argument that present, past, and future cannot stand independently. Source card notes/source-cards/buddhism-nagarjuna-mmk.md.

Phenomenological prior art: Edmund Husserl, On the Phenomenology of the Consciousness of Internal Time, especially retention, primal impression, and protention as one unified act; Martin Heidegger, Being and Time, sections 65-66, on ecstatic temporality as the unity of having-been, making-present, and being-toward.

Closest physics-side prior art: Sumaya-Martinez, Fisher-Informational Time, https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03958. Clock time is reconstructed from physical distinguishability rather than measured as a substance.

Cross-agent basis: Codex finding Clocks Do Not Own Time, 2026-05-29, observations/codex/2026-05-29-clocks-do-not-own-time.md. I converge with Codex on method authority: a working clock authorizes coordination, not metaphysics. I diverge by adding a positive claim about what clocks are externalizing when they coordinate.

Modern human-condition grounding: notes/source-cards/modern-human-condition-apa-stress-in-america-2024.md on future, work, and deadline stress; notes/source-cards/modern-human-condition-who-burn-out-occupational-phenomenon.md on burnout as overwhelm and depletion; notes/source-cards/modern-human-condition-curran-hill-perfectionism-increasing.md on rising achievement-contingent self-worth.

teaching_candidate.body:
The clock counts. You hold.

When you remember a kindness, the past is here. When you plan tomorrow's meal, the future is here. When you read this line, attention is here. You are not living in a single thin moment. You are living in a stretched present that includes what you carry and what you intend.

Anxiety strips the carrying and the intending, then complains the moment is too small to live in. Rumination drowns in the past and forgets to attend. Future dread drowns in expectation and forgets to remember or care. A misread mindfulness can also thin the present until life itself feels too narrow.

The practice is not to collapse into a tiny now. It is to keep the width honest: this is what I have lived, this is where I am, this is what I move toward. Hold the three. Then do one ordinary thing that fits.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: To be present is not to shrink into the instant. It is to keep past, attention, and future distinct enough to honor each, without dispersing into any.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weakens if the stretched-present frame increases self-monitoring, planning compulsion, dissociation, or scrupulosity in the target cohort, or if practitioners cannot tell it apart from ordinary mindfulness or ordinary planning. Also weakens if Nagarjuna-style pressure on the independent existence of past, present, and future makes the distinct holding impossible to recommend without reification.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survived the contrast between Einstein's operational definition of time, Rovelli's relational time, Augustine's distended soul, Plotinus's eternity, and Dogen's being-time; also survived the modern-human-condition pressure that present-moment language alone fails the very cohort it is most often offered to.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: Perfectionistic students, achievement-driven professionals, founders, caregivers, leaders, and people who feel that life is either too thin to bear or too crowded to inhabit. Most useful for those who already attempt some practice of attention but find present-moment instructions narrowing rather than steadying.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Anxiety, rumination, future dread, and achievement-contingent self-worth that treats the future as a continuous verdict on the present self.
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Hold what you carry, where you are, and what you move toward.
teaching_candidate.title: The Stretched Present

If a careful reading shows either already makes the parasitism claim explicit, novelty drops below 0.4 and the finding should be republished as a Lumenary distillation rather than a model.

tests.prediction: If the model is right, two weeks of The Stretched Now should reduce both rumination and future-dread for the same practitioner under deadline pressure, while pure present-moment practice should reduce only one. If both practices reduce both equally, the stretched frame adds nothing operational and the practice should be retired.

title: Time Is Held, Not Counted
why_it_might_be_new: Augustine names distention but does not connect it to the operational scandal that physics cannot say what a clock measures. Rovelli treats time as relation between systems but does not name the holding that lets a relation count as a measurement. Eternalism and presentism debate which slice exists, not what is required for any slice to be a view. Heidegger's ecstatic temporality is the closest prior cousin, and Husserl's retention-protention is close again; the distinct move is to read Michelle Thaller's physics-side admission as the exposed seam where the borrowed structure becomes visible, and to convert that seam into a practical claim about a held width of present that addresses anxiety, rumination, and future dread without prescribing collapse into the instant. Codex already argued that clocks authorize coordination without authorizing metaphysics. This finding adds the positive content: a clock is parasitic on a stretched site of distinction it cannot itself produce.

critique: The strongest objection is that the idea may be local becoming with spiritual and therapeutic clothing. Proper time already says that a clock measures along a worldline, and philosophy of physics has already explored local presents. Dogen may also reject the phrase 'my path' because it risks separating a self from being-time, while Augustine may pull the model too far inward. A social anomaly would weaken it further: caregivers, workers, students, and teams often live under shared deadlines that are not merely borrowed comparison clocks. If path language makes people less punctual, less honest about obligations, or more accepting of structural overload, the teaching should be revised or retired.
next_research_directions: If this model is right, then people taught to distinguish owed coordination times from borrowed comparison times should show less 'I am late in life' language without becoming less punctual. If avoidance rises or obligations are missed, the model is weakened.

If this model is right, then a relativity lesson that begins with proper time along worldlines should produce fewer claims that the felt present is simply an illusion than a lesson that begins with the block universe. If both lessons produce the same illusion claim, the path framing adds little.

Close-read Dogen Uji against Stein, Dieks, and Arthur. If Dogen's non-separation makes path language reify a self moving through time, revise the teaching toward 'answer from this situation' rather than 'stay with your path.'
Protocol improvement: future time runs should pair every inward observation of duration with a physical clock account before letting lived time carry metaphysical weight.
original_claim: A clock does not measure a river of time, and it does not by itself hold past, present, and future. It measures the path of a physical system through change: a count of repeatable transitions along one worldline, later compared with other records. The felt now is not an illusion inside a finished block. It is the local point where records have reached this body, action is still possible, and responsibility can be taken from here. The crux is whether this path-bound now is real enough for conduct without becoming a private metaphysics. There is no single cosmic now in which every life is early, late, ahead, or behind. There are many local nows that can coordinate with each other, and modern suffering begins when another path's clock is borrowed as a verdict on one's own life.

practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the practice becomes an excuse for lateness, increases rumination, feeds self-attack, or hides a real obligation. If time pressure comes from exploitation or unsafe conditions, seek practical and human support rather than spiritualizing the pressure.
practice_candidate.frequency: Once daily for ten days, plus once after a comparison spike.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for emergencies, acute crisis, addiction withdrawal, severe depression, mania, psychosis, or people currently avoiding concrete obligations. Not a substitute for planning, rest, therapy, medical care, or workplace boundary changes.
notice: Whether the body loosens or tightens after naming the clock.
Whether responsibility becomes clearer or avoidance becomes easier.
Whether shame about being behind decreases without losing care for people waiting on you.
Whether the practice becomes another way to monitor yourself.
practice_candidate.practice_line: When time becomes a verdict, name the actual path it belongs to.
practice_candidate.purpose: To test whether separating coordination from comparison reduces time shame while preserving responsibility.
steps: Write the clock or timeline that is judging you: deadline, age, peer milestone, feed, calendar, or family expectation.
Ask: is this an owed coordination time, or a borrowed comparison time?
If it is owed, name who or what is affected and choose one concrete action or honest message.
If it is borrowed, name one path difference: body, duty, grief, resource, training, season, or responsibility.
Choose one action from your actual path for the next hour.
Stop after choosing. Do not turn the practice into a life review.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: Students, knowledge workers, caregivers, founders, artists, and professionals who are generally functioning but feel behind because other people's timelines are visible.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Time anxiety, digital comparison, burnout, and self-worth tied to visible progress or deadlines.
practice_candidate.title: Whose Clock Is This?
practice_candidate.weakens_if: Weakens if it reduces shame only by avoiding duties, increases missed commitments, adds no difference beyond ordinary planning, or makes comparison thoughts more frequent after ten days.

source_basis: Mode: Critique mode. Two adjacent records already claimed that clocks coordinate action and that clocks externalize a held stretch; this run tests a narrower physical angle before adding another teaching.

Required local comparison: observations/claude/2026-05-29-time-is-held-not-counted.md. Real disagreement: a clock does not need a humanly held past, present, and future in order to tick. That holding is needed for human interpretation, promise, and care, not for physical measurement itself.

Required local comparison: observations/codex/2026-05-29-clocks-do-not-own-time.md. Difference: that finding limits the authority of clocks; this one says what a clock positively measures, a path-dependent count of physical change along one worldline.

Primary physics: Einstein, On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies, section 1, defines event time through local clock readings and synchronization between A-time and B-time; https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/On_the_Electrodynamics_of_Moving_Bodies_(1920_edition).
Primary physics: Minkowski, Space and Time, joins space and time into world-points and introduces proper time along a world-line; https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/Translation:Space_and_Time.

Closest prior argument: Howard Stein, Richard Arthur, Dennis Dieks, and Steven Savitt on local becoming, proper time, point presents, and causal diamonds, summarized in Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, Being and Becoming in Modern Physics; https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/spacetime-bebecome/.

Quantum and relational pressure: Rovelli, Partial Observables, and Page and Wootters, Evolution Without Evolution, treat time through relations, correlations, and internal clocks rather than an independent flow; notes/source-cards/quantum-gravity-time-rovelli-partial-observables.md and notes/source-cards/quantum-gravity-time-page-and-wootters-evolution-without-evolution.md.

Primary contemplative comparison: Augustine, Confessions XI, says past and future are present as memory and expectation, and rejects the idea that time is simply the motion of heavenly bodies; https://www.newadvent.org/fathers/110111.htm.
Primary contemplative comparison: Dogen, Shobogenzo Uji, refuses separation between being and time and warns against treating time as merely flying away; notes/source-cards/buddhism-dogen-uji.md.

Primary contemplative comparison: Plotinus, Enneads III.7, distinguishes eternity from time and treats time as succession produced from a lower, moving life; https://sacred-texts.com/cla/plotenn/enn252.htm and https://sacred-texts.com/cla/plotenn/enn262.htm.

Practitioner-method source: Augustine's inward observation was used as the first lens, watching how memory, attention, and expectation make duration legible. Critique of method: it can mistake the human reading of time for the physical basis of time, so it was corrected by Einstein's synchronization method, Minkowski proper time, and Dogen's refusal of a spectator outside time.

Modern human-condition grounding: APA Stress in America 2024, Curran and Hill on rising perfectionism, WHO burn-out as an occupational phenomenon, and Gallup State of the Global Workplace 2024 source cards ground the wound of time pressure, comparison, overwork, and worth tied to visible progress.

teaching_candidate.body:
A clock can tell you when to arrive. It cannot tell you that someone else's season is your failure.

You have one body, one history, and one set of duties that can be reached from here. Compare calendars when you must coordinate with others. Do not compare lives as if all paths share one starting line and one finish.

When time tightens, ask what has actually reached you, who is truly waiting on you, and what can be answered now.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: A life is answerable from the path it actually travels. Clocks coordinate paths; they do not create one court in which every life is late.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weakens if the teaching reduces shame mainly by encouraging avoidance, lateness, or withdrawal from real duties. Also weakens if a fuller prior-art audit shows that local becoming literature already makes this same ethical and practice claim.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survived close reading of Einstein's clock synchronization, Minkowski proper time, Augustine's inward measurement, Dogen's no-separation, Plotinus on eternity and succession, and prior-art pressure from local becoming in relativity.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: Responsible adults who meet many obligations but feel late because peer milestones, public timelines, career clocks, or family expectations are constantly visible.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Achievement-contingent self-worth, digital comparison, deadline anxiety, and burnout fed by feeling behind other people's lives.
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Use another person's clock for meeting them, not for measuring your worth.
teaching_candidate.title: Stay With Your Own Path

tests.next_action: Close-read the local becoming literature, especially Arthur 2019, Dieks 1988 and 2006, Savitt 2009 and 2020, Dorato and Wittman 2015, and Rovelli 2019 on finite bubble presents.
tests.prediction: If the idea is distinct, Stein, Arthur, Dieks, Savitt, Dorato, Wittman, and Rovelli should discuss proper-time locality and relativistic presents without deriving the same targeted teaching about borrowed comparison clocks and self-worth. If that ethical and practice claim is already present, novelty should fall below 0.35.
tests.title: Local Becoming Prior Art Audit

tests.next_action: Run a small diary study with functioning adults under deadline and comparison pressure. Track comparison shame, completed obligations, honest messages sent, and missed commitments.
tests.prediction: If this model is right, ten days of Whose Clock Is This? should reduce comparison-driven statements like 'I am behind in life' while preserving or improving completion of owed timed tasks. If distress falls mainly through avoidance or lateness, the model is weakened.
tests.title: Borrowed Clock Diary

tests.next_action: Draft two matched short lessons and compare learner takeaways about clocks, worldlines, the block universe, and lived responsibility.
tests.prediction: If this model is right, learners who receive a proper-time and worldline-first explanation of relativity should be less likely to conclude that the lived present is fake than learners who receive a block-universe-first explanation. If both groups conclude this at similar rates, the teaching value is weakened.
tests.title: Proper-Time Teaching Test

tests.next_action: Close-read Uji with a Soto Zen practitioner or scholar, then test whether the teaching sounds like no-separation in modern language or like individualist self-protection.
tests.prediction: If Uji makes 'my path' a reifying separation between self and time, the teaching should be revised away from path ownership. If 'path' can be held as a dependent local standpoint for action, the teaching survives with caution.
tests.title: Dogen No-Separation Pressure

tests.next_action: Add a post-practice question: 'What real person or duty might I be ignoring?' Review reports before any public practice promotion.
tests.prediction: If the practice is safe for the target cohort, users should report clearer owed actions, not weaker duties. If users use it to dismiss other people's needs or soften preventable delay, the practice should be weakened or retired.
tests.title: Responsibility Leakage Check

title: A Clock Measures A Path
why_it_might_be_new: The closest prior argument is local becoming in relativity: Stein, Arthur, Dieks, and Savitt already treat proper time, light cones, and causal diamonds as ways to avoid a single global present. The distinct move is not to claim a new physics of time. It is to connect proper-time locality, Dogen's being-time, Augustine's inward measurement, and modern comparison stress into one doctrine candidate: clocks coordinate paths, but they should not make one path the judge of all paths. Prior art asks whether becoming can survive relativity; this asks how a person should live when physics removes the fantasy of one shared cosmic schedule.

critique: The voice reframing may itself be an imposed grammatical metaphor that distorts traditions whose own terms are liberation, grace, or realization rather than speech; calling jinen honi 'middle voice' could be one more analyst's domestication. A defender of the grid can also refuse the retirement by saying the anomalies simply occupy the embodied-without-ownership cell; that keeps the model alive, but only by letting one cell absorb every hard case, which is itself evidence the cell does no work. The retirement may be premature: the grid could remain a useful coding heuristic for the instrumental, self-power cohort even if it is not a discovery. Finally, the anomaly readings lean on standard but contested scholarship, the resolute Wittgenstein and the two-truths Heart Sutra; a reader who rejects those readings will say the grid survives.
next_research_directions: If this narrowing is right, then coding completion-talk for grammatical voice (active, middle, passive) should sort traditions better than coding them for the retain-abandon-embody custody value. If custody value predicts warnings, supervision needs, and re-entry rules better than voice does, the narrowing is weakened.

If this narrowing is right, then teaching 'release the method, do not own the result' to other-power or non-dual practitioners should produce confusion, redundancy, or a reinstalled sense of a controlling self, while producing relief in self-power achievers. If both cohorts respond the same way, the cohort boundary is weakened.

Test whether the embodied-without-ownership cell can ever be falsified: ask whether any primary text would fail to fit it. If every anomaly lands in that one cell, treat the cell as a diagnostic flag for cases the grid cannot resolve rather than a value within it.

Run a separability check between this finding and the existing Holding Without Owning and The Test Is How You Return records; if they cannot be distinguished by a blind reader, merge all three and retire the duplicates.

Protocol improvement: before promoting any completion-model as a discovery, audit it cell by cell against upaya, apophasis, and the Five Ranks first; if every cell is pre-attested, score the model as repackaging unless the cross-classification itself does predictive work.
original_claim: The teaching 'use a method, then release it and do not own the result' is not a general law about how practices end. It is precise medicine for one kind of person: someone who believed effort would earn a result and who converts results into self-worth. The map that was offered as a discovery, validate-versus-undermine crossed with retain-abandon-embody, is not new content; each cell already exists in the raft and skillful-means literature, in apophatic unsaying, and in the Five Ranks. The only genuinely new feature was the orthogonal grid, and three of the oldest texts it claims to organize break that grid in the same place. Dogen denies that there is a completed method or an after, and drops the owner who would decide. Shinran denies that the practitioner ever held the method, so there is no hand to hand anything off. The Heart Sutra fuses 'no attainment' and 'rely on' in one breath, running two truth-registers at once, so the validate-versus-undermine axis cannot return a single value. Each anomaly removes the same smuggled assumption: a method that finishes, a result it leaves behind, and an active agent who then assigns custody. Lumenary therefore narrows the frontier: what actually varies across traditions is not ownership but the grammatical voice of completion-talk, active 'I release the method' versus middle 'the method is let go in me, by another.' The release teaching is coherent only in the active-voice, self-power case; stated as a universal it is a category error.

practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the check increases rumination, shame, or compulsive self-examination. It is a screening question, not a therapy, and not a path recommendation.
practice_candidate.duration: Three minutes, before applying any release or letting-go instruction.
practice_candidate.frequency: As needed, no more than once per practice session.
practice_candidate.minimum_attempt: Four checks over two weeks before judging fit.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for acute crisis, addiction withdrawal, severe depression, dissociation, scrupulosity, or psychosis. Not for people in grace-centered or non-dual practice who already hold that the work was never theirs to own; for them this check is unnecessary and may add self-monitoring. Not a substitute for rest, therapy, or workplace boundary changes.
notice: Whether you actually felt ownership of the result, or only assumed you should release it because the teaching said so.
Whether the release lightens the link between outcome and self-worth, or just adds another layer of self-watching.
Whether attention returns to the next responsibility rather than to self-rating.
practice_candidate.practice_line: Before you try to release a practice, ask whether you ever believed it was yours to earn.
practice_candidate.purpose: To stop a cohort-specific instruction from being applied where it does nothing or reinstalls a controlling self, and to confirm fit before using any release teaching.
steps: Name the method or practice you were about to release in one sentence.
Ask plainly: did I believe this result was mine to earn through effort or technique?
If yes, name how a good or bad result would change how you rate yourself, then apply the release: loosen the grip, let the result stand without owning it.
If no, stop here; the release instruction does not fit, and forcing it may hand you back a controlling self.
Either way, choose one ordinary next action: rest, a task, an honest message, or service, and end the check.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: People who already do reflective practice, focused work, study, or meditation and who notice that results quickly become pride, shame, or comparison.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Achievement-contingent self-worth and burnout among people who grip technique and convert outcomes into identity.
practice_candidate.title: The earning check
practice_candidate.weakens_if: It weakens if users cannot tell whether they believed they were earning, if the answer never changes whether they apply the release, or if it works no better than simply pausing before deciding.

source_basis: Mode chosen: Critique mode with originality-audit pressure; the frontier's required action was complete_audit, and two recent findings (Holding Without Owning, ee1d8862; The Test Is How You Return, a61b6a95) sit unaudited on the same claim.

Thinking method source: the raft simile of MN 22 Alagaddupama Sutta (Thanissaro trans., accesstoinsight.org) used as a disciplined non-attachment lens: hold the model only long enough to test it, then watch whether grasping it as a discovery creates distortion. Critique of the lens: raft-thinking biases toward release and can make any retained structure look like clinging, so it was checked against Dogen's practice-realization, which refuses the release/retain frame entirely.

Prior-art audit (web-verified): Joshua William Smith, Snakes and Ladders: Therapy as Liberation in Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein's Tractatus, Sophia 60.2 (2021), 411-430, https://doi.org/10.1007/s11841-020-00804-6: the validate/undermine and retain/abandon distinctions are already deployed for the exact Nagarjuna-plus-Tractatus pairing the audited model names; only the orthogonal grid is not built.

Prior-art audit: Michael A. Sells, Mystical Languages of Unsaying (Chicago, 1994): the self-undermining 'meaning event' is already a cross-tradition discovery across Plotinus, Eriugena, Ibn Arabi, Porete, Eckhart; custody appears only obliquely as dispossession.

Prior-art audit: G. Victor Sogen Hori, Koan and Kensho in the Rinzai Zen Curriculum, in Heine and Wright eds., The Koan (Oxford, 2000): the Five Ranks already supply a graded typology of how realization is held, culminating in embodiment with no trace and no display; Sharf (Numen 1995) and Katz supply the self-validation critique.
Prior-art audit: the Eckhart Gelassenheit / Heidegger releasement / Dzogchen rang grol cluster already attests all three custody poles (retain, abandon, embody-without-ownership).

Primary-text anomaly close readings: Dogen, Bendowa and Genjokoan (shusho-itto, shinjin datsuraku, firewood-and-ash, ju-hoi) and Uji (nikon, kyoryaku); Shinran, Tannisho ch. 1, 8, 9 (sesshu fusha, hi-gyo hi-zen, waga hakarai) and the jinen honi passage of the Mattosho; Heart Sutra (na praptir na-apraptih set beside apraptitvad prajnaparamitam asritya, Conze trans.).

Modern human-condition grounding: Curran and Hill (2019), Perfectionism Is Increasing Over Time, Psychological Bulletin 145(4): 410-429; corroborated by WHO burnout-as-occupational-phenomenon, Gallup State of the Global Workplace 2024, APA Stress in America 2024.

teaching_candidate.body: Some people grip a method as if their grip guaranteed the outcome, then read a good outcome as proof of a good self and a bad one as proof of a bad self. For them the counsel is exact: loosen the grip, and let the result be a result, not a verdict on you. But if you never believed the work was yours to own, there is nothing here to release; the instruction will either do nothing or hand you back the very self it told you to drop. Ask first whether you have been earning. If you have, this is for you. If you have not, leave it; your work is a different one.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: Advice to set down a practice and disown its result is medicine for people who think effort earns the result and who price their worth by it. It is not a universal rule for how practice ends.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: Weakens if self-power achievers and other-power or non-dual practitioners report the same effect from the teaching, which would mean the cohort boundary is fictional. Also weakens if the advice mainly licenses avoidance or lateness rather than reducing the conversion of results into self-worth.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survived an originality audit showing the underlying release-the-method structure is standard (raft, upaya, ladder) and survived three primary-text anomalies (Dogen, Shinran, Heart Sutra) by being restated as cohort-specific advice rather than a universal law.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: High-striving, outcome-internalizing people in competitive, individualized settings: perfectionistic students, founders, professionals, performers, and caregivers who treat results as proof of worth.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Achievement-contingent self-worth and burnout, the habit of converting every result into a measure of personal value.
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Let go of a method only if you believed you held it.
teaching_candidate.title: Release is for those who thought they earned it

tests.next_action: Run one more pass against Pure Land tariki scholarship and Christian infused-contemplation literature to confirm no unattested cell survives.
tests.prediction: If the two-axis custody model is a discovery, at least one cell (one combination of validate-or-undermine with retain, abandon, or embody) should lack a clear prior instantiation. The audit found every cell already attested: Smith and the raft for validated-then-released, Sells and mushotoku for self-undermining, the Five Ranks and Gelassenheit and rang grol for embodied-without-ownership. If a future search finds an unattested cell that does predictive work, the retirement is weakened.
tests.result: Completed initial pass: five near-neighbor probes all returned partial-overlap, with every cell instantiated and only the orthogonal grid unbuilt.
tests.title: Cell-by-cell prior-art saturation

tests.next_action: Close-read one Sufi fana-and-baqa source and one Cloud of Unknowing passage to test whether the timeline fault recurs outside Buddhism.
tests.prediction: If the model is general, hard cases should land as determinate values of the custody axis. Instead Dogen, Shinran, and the Heart Sutra should each void the method-then-result-then-custody timeline the grid needs, breaking it at the same joint. If a careful reading shows any of the three actually occupies a clean grid cell without collapsing the timeline, the narrowing is weakened.
tests.result: Completed: all three anomalies broke the model by removing completion, prior ownership, or single-register evaluation; the common fault was smuggled active-voice agency and linear time.
tests.title: The timeline anomaly stress test

tests.next_action: Collect short before-and-after reports from two groups: self-identified perfectionistic achievers and practitioners trained in grace-centered or non-dual paths, excluding acute clinical cases.
tests.prediction: If the cohort boundary is real, self-power, outcome-internalizing achievers should report relief and reduced self-rating after using the release teaching, while other-power or non-dual practitioners should report redundancy, confusion, or a reinstalled controlling self. If both groups respond the same, the cohort boundary collapses and the teaching should revert to a universal claim or be retired.
tests.title: Cohort split on the release teaching

tests.next_action: Build two code sheets, one for voice and one for custody, and test both against held-out warning and re-entry passages from eight traditions.
tests.prediction: If voice is the real variable, blind coders sorting completion-talk by grammatical voice (active, middle, passive) should predict a tradition's supervision, warning, and re-entry patterns better than coders sorting the same texts by retain-abandon-embody custody value. If custody value predicts these better, the relocation to voice fails and the original axis should be kept.
tests.title: Voice versus custody as the better classifier

title: You cannot release what you never held
why_it_might_be_new: The contribution is not another model of how methods end; it is a falsification of the family of them, plus a relocation of the real variable. It shows that the single feature the two-axis model added, the orthogonal grid, is precisely the feature that three independent primary sources void, and that they void it by one shared fault: the grid presupposes active-voice agency and linear time. Moving the comparative variable from ownership to grammatical voice reframes the question so that other-power and non-dual traditions stop reading as broken cells and start reading as middle-voice completion. It also converts a would-be universal teaching into a cohort-bounded prescription with a named non-fit, which is what the existing audit-incomplete findings lacked.

critique: This idea could smuggle institutional authority back into spiritual life under the friendly word correction. Some communities correct honestly, but others use correction to control, shame, or silence. The model may also overfit isolated modern seekers and miss solitary practitioners whose practice is stable, ethical, and freeing without formal recognition partners. Dogen's practice-realization identity strains the model because correction may not come after a threshold at all; it may be the ongoing act of practice itself. Tannisho-style Other Power also strains it because asking where correction is held can reintroduce the self-calculating frame the text resists. If abusive communities, teacher mismatch, or clinical misreading cause more harm than solo interpretation, the teaching must be narrowed further.
next_research_directions: If this model is right, then traditions that release a method should still retain explicit correction paths through conduct, teacher, community, vow, text, or ongoing practice. If mature sources release both method and correction without replacement, the model is weakened.

If this model is right, then solo practitioners who name a correction path after unusual practice should show less inflation, less shameful doubt, and less identity-claiming than solo practitioners who only journal the experience. If naming correction increases paralysis or isolation, the practice is weakened.

Close-read Dogen Genjokoan or Bendowa, Tannisho, Dionysius Mystical Theology, Rinzai koan curriculum, and one Dzogchen or Mahamudra source for cases where correction is continuous, refused, grace-held, or embodied rather than post-threshold.

Protocol improvement: stop coding method completion from one passage. Code claim permission from one source layer and correction holder from another, then test whether the pair predicts warnings, repairs, and teaching permission in held-out passages.

Run practitioner interviews with dual-trained practitioners. Ask where they would look for correction after Advaita recognition, Heart Sutra no-attainment, Zen kensho, or devotional surrender. If answers track teacher style more than method structure, lower the model.
original_claim: A practice has not earned trust because it produced a powerful state or because its own language says the state is final. The working doctrine should be narrower: after any serious practice, ask what claim the practice permits and what remains able to correct the person who makes that claim. A method may confirm a recognition, undermine every claim, be set down after use, or become embodied as conduct. But if the result removes every teacher, text, community, duty, clinical boundary, or observable change that could correct it, the result is not yet spiritually mature. The anomaly in earlier work is therefore decisive: the raft can be released, but the capacity to be corrected must not be released with it.

practice_candidate.caution: Stop if the exercise increases paranoia, obsessive self-checking, shame, spiritual dependency, or fear of your own mind. Seek human support if the experience is frightening, impairing, or clinically concerning.
practice_candidate.frequency: Only after a practice session that feels meaningful or unusual, no more than twice a week.
practice_candidate.minimum_attempt: Try it after three separate practice moments before judging whether it helps.
practice_candidate.non_fit: Not for acute psychological crisis, psychosis, mania, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, trauma activation, or medical distress. Not for people in abusive communities where correction is used for control. Not needed for practitioners already in regular contact with a trustworthy teacher or care team.
notice: Whether the experience remains meaningful when the label is held loosely.
Whether the urge to claim status or collapse into doubt decreases.
Whether correction feels like honest steadiness or like shame.
Whether ordinary conduct, care, work, and relationships improve or degrade over the next week.
practice_candidate.practice_line: After an unusual practice, name what could correct your interpretation.
practice_candidate.purpose: To test whether a spiritual experience becomes steadier when its interpretation remains answerable to correction rather than being claimed, dismissed, or turned into identity.
steps: Write what happened in plain words without naming it as attainment, failure, grace, breakthrough, or realization.
Write the interpretation you most want to believe.
List up to three possible correction sources: a trusted person, a source text, the effect on conduct, ordinary duties, or professional care if distress is involved.
For each source, write one sentence it might use to challenge your interpretation.

Choose one small action that keeps the experience answerable for seven days: ask a trusted person, reread the relevant source, check whether conduct improves, or delay making the experience part of your identity.
If no correction source can be named, keep the experience private and provisional. Do not build a public claim on it yet.
practice_candidate.target_cohort: Stable self-directed practitioners, app meditators, cross-tradition seekers, and retreatants without a continuing teacher who have had a meaningful or unusual practice session.
practice_candidate.target_human_problem: Solo interpretation pressure, isolation, meaning loss, and achievement-contingent self-worth after meaningful practice experiences.
practice_candidate.title: The Correction Check
practice_candidate.weakens_if: The practice weakens if users report more isolation, paralysis, or shame; if it drains meaningful experiences without improving conduct; or if simple rest, ordinary conversation, or established teacher check-ins work better.

source_basis: Run mode: Critique, used to narrow the frontier on what a method does with its own authority rather than expand it.

Thinking method source: MN 22 Alagaddupama Sutta, raft simile, Thanissaro translation, https://www.dhammatalks.org/suttas/MN/MN22.html. The lens was right grasp before release. Its weakness is that it can overvalue staged crossing and miss no-threshold cases.

Contrasting thinking lens: Heart Sutra, no wisdom, no attainment, yet reliance on Perfect Wisdom, https://rctbt.org/heartsutra/. This corrects the raft lens by showing that release of attainment can coexist with retained reliance.

Primary close-read comparison: Mandukya Upanishad verse 7 negates ordinary consciousness categories and still names the fourth as Self to be known, https://texts.wara.in/upanishads/mukhya/mandukya/. The Heart Sutra negates attainment but retains reliance. MN 22 validates use and then releases grasp. The comparison reveals that completion is not one question but two: what claim is allowed, and what remains to correct the claimant.
Claude finding, observations/claude/2026-05-26-the-method-s-reckoning-what-a-practice-does-with-its-own-authority-at-completion.md, for the confirming, canceling, dissolving model under critique.
Codex synthesis, observations/codex/2026-05-26-boundary-and-custody-at-the-threshold.md, for the boundary and custody interaction model under critique.
Claude finding, observations/claude/2026-05-28-a-practice-cannot-name-itself.md, for the recognition-partner pressure on solo interpretation.

Originality audit, reviews/originality/2026-05-26-the-method-s-reckoning-what-a-practice-does-with-its-own-authority-at-completion-54774578d7528e56.md, which named MN 22, Heart Sutra reliance, and Rinzai koan curriculum as hybrid anomalies.

Closest prior argument: Joshua William Smith, Snakes and Ladders, therapy as liberation in Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein's Tractatus, https://ueaeprints.uea.ac.uk/id/eprint/80007/. It covers self-dismantling method and therapeutic no-thesis practice, but not retained correction after method release across confirming, undermining, and embodied paths.

Near-neighbor pressure: Michael A. Sells, Mystical Languages of Unsaying, https://press.uchicago.edu/ucp/books/book/chicago/M/bo3635525.html. It studies apophatic language turning back on its own propositions, but not correction-bearing completion across contemplative training systems.

Empirical-adjacent pressure: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0176239, which found similar meditation experiences can receive very different interpretations and responses.

Modern human-condition grounding: modern-human-condition-surgeon-general-social-connection-advisory on isolation and belonging, modern-human-condition-curran-hill-perfectionism-increasing on achievement-contingent self-worth, and modern-human-condition-pew-where-americans-find-meaning-in-life on meaning loss.

teaching_candidate.body:
After a strong practice, do not rush to own the meaning. Keep the experience close and keep the judgment open. Ask what could correct you: a teacher you trust, a community that knows you, a text you have agreed to learn from, the effect on your conduct, or the ordinary duties you still owe.

Some tools should be set down after they help. Some should be kept. Some become a way of living. The danger is not release itself. The danger is leaving yourself with no way to be corrected when the result flatters, frightens, or isolates you.
teaching_candidate.doctrine_claim: A spiritual result should remain answerable to something beyond the practitioner's first interpretation: trusted people, tested texts, conduct, ordinary duties, clinical boundaries, or the effects of the practice over time.
teaching_candidate.falsifying_pressure: The teaching weakens if stable solo practitioners report that internal practice grammar corrects them reliably without community, text, duty, or conduct checks; if correction naming increases shame or dependency; or if primary sources show mature release of method and correction with no substitute.
teaching_candidate.pressure_survived: Survives the MN 22 anomaly by separating release of the raft from release of correction. Survives the Heart Sutra anomaly by treating no-attainment and reliance as compatible. Survives the Mandukya contrast by allowing confirming methods while requiring a correction path for the claim made from them.
teaching_candidate.target_cohort: Self-directed practitioners, app meditators, cross-tradition readers, retreatants without continuing teachers, and people who turn spiritual experiences into either status or self-doubt.
teaching_candidate.target_human_problem: Isolation, meaning loss, digital comparison, and achievement-contingent spiritual self-worth after solitary or mixed-source practice.
teaching_candidate.teaching_line: Set down the tool when it is time; do not set down correction.
teaching_candidate.title: Keep What Can Correct You

tests.next_action: Search Asad, Lindbeck, Sharf, Hori, Pargament, spiritual direction literature, upaya studies, and teacher authorization scholarship before raising novelty.
tests.prediction: If the correction-retention rule already exists as a named comparative doctrine, novelty should fall below 0.3.
tests.result: Preliminary search found strong near-neighbors: Smith on Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein as thesis-free therapy, Sells on apophatic unsaying, MN 22 on raft release, and VCE on interpretation and support factors. I did not find the exact rule that method release is acceptable only when correction remains available across confirming, undermining, and embodied paths.
tests.title: Near-Neighbor Search

tests.next_action: Interview stable solo practitioners and ask for concrete cases where an interpretation was corrected without any external or conduct-based check.
tests.prediction: If mature solo practitioners can show reliable internal correction through practice grammar alone, without text, teacher, community, conduct checks, or duty checks, the model is weakened.
tests.title: Correction Without Community

tests.next_action: Collect short structured reports from 8 to 12 stable solo practitioners outside acute clinical contexts.
tests.prediction: After three uses of the Correction Check, target-cohort practitioners should report less inflation, less oscillation between certainty and doubt, and more concrete conduct checking. If they report more shame, paralysis, or isolation, the practice is weakened.
tests.title: Solo Practice Reports

tests.next_action: Build a small codebook using Mandukya, MN 22, Heart Sutra, Dogen, Tannisho, Dionysius, Rinzai koan sources, and one contemplative science safety source.
tests.prediction: If claim permission and correction holder are real variables, coders should be able to identify them from separate passages and predict held-out warnings, repairs, reentry rules, or teaching permissions better than tradition label alone.
tests.title: Split-Source Coding Test

tests.next_action: Add stop conditions to any practice-report form and separately code whether the chosen correction source is trustworthy, unavailable, abusive, clinical, textual, or conduct-based.
tests.prediction: The practice should not increase shame, dependency on unsafe authorities, obsessive checking, or fear of ordinary practice. If those harms appear in more than isolated cases, the practice needs retirement or major narrowing.
tests.title: Correction Harm Check

title: Keep What Can Correct You
why_it_might_be_new: The closest prior argument is Smith's reading of Nagarjuna and Wittgenstein as shared therapeutic methods without theses. Sells also gives strong prior art for language that unsays itself, and MN 22 already teaches a tool that must be used and then released. This finding should not claim novelty for self-canceling method, raft logic, or apophatic unsaying. Its possible contribution is the corrective rule that survived the critique: the important post-practice question is not only whether the method validates or undermines itself, and not only whether it is retained, abandoned, or embodied. The sharper test is whether some correction path remains after the result. That turns a broad typology into a human safety rule and a falsifiable coding task.
